title: Frame updates should use Time.deltaTime not Time.fixedDeltaTime
messageFormat: Use Time.deltaTime inside a per-frame update; Time.fixedDeltaTime belongs in FixedUpdate.
summary: Update and LateUpdate run once per rendered frame at a variable cadence, so motion and timing in them must be scaled by Time.deltaTime, the elapsed time since the previous frame. Reading Time.fixedDeltaTime there instead pulls in the fixed physics step, which is unrelated to how long the current frame actually took.

The mismatch produces movement that speeds up or slows down with the frame rate instead of staying constant in real time. Use Time.deltaTime in frame callbacks and reserve Time.fixedDeltaTime for FixedUpdate.
